$comment: CURATED REGISTRY for the board — the static half of board.json. NOT board.json itself: it carries emit-only extensions the output schema forbids (model.match, bench.emit, harness_variants). scripts/update-sweep-status.sh --emit-json reads this, joins S3 measurements onto it, strips the emit-only fields, and writes a schema-conformant board.json. Keep models[] in sync with SWEEP_ROSTER/FRONTIER_ROSTER in the script (same models, board-display metadata added here). Edit canonical_condition only with a citation in docs/research/benchmark-canonical-protocols.md.
$comment_harness_variants: Data-driven harness-condition mapping (emit-only). To add a NEW harness on ANY bench and show its uplift: (1) add the new value to condition_dims.harness.values; (2) add a rule here mapping the run's extra.harness_variant string to that value; (3) set emit.harness_axis=true on the bench. No emitter code change — the <ISSUE> schema gate flags a value you forgot to declare. RULE CONTRACT: each rule needs both `match` and a string `value`. `match` is a jq test() regex matched UNANCHORED (substring) against the raw harness_variant.variant string, and rules are tried IN ORDER with first-match-wins — so put specific patterns before broad ones, and anchor (e.g. ^<ISSUE>$) when a new variant could be a substring of an existing rule. A run with no harness_variant maps to 'stock'; an unmatched non-empty variant passes through verbatim (so it surfaces and the gate tells you to declare it).

models.name: Qwen3 235B-A22B Thinking
models.profile: AWQ-INT4 · vLLM 0.21 · TP=8 · 256K ctx · native thinking

models.name: Opus 4.7
models.profile: Bedrock us-east-1 cross-region · direct-API for SEC-bench (CVP-approved)

benches.title: HumanEval+
benches.description: Expanded HumanEval with hardened tests. Authors (EvalPlus, NeurIPS 2023) pin greedy pass@1 but specify NO thinking mode (predates reasoning models) — so canonical_condition is omitted on our axes. thinking=off (ᵗ) is a factual label, not a deviation-from-canonical.

benches.title: IFEval
benches.description: Prompt-level strict instruction-following (lm-eval-harness prompt_level_strict_acc). Authors pin greedy / strict-accuracy, NO thinking mode → canonical_condition omitted. Note: thinking-on can LOWER strict-accuracy here (visible think-blocks violate output-format instructions).

benches.title: BCB-Hard
benches.description: BigCodeBench Hard subset (Instruct split, calibrated pass@1) via the bigcodebench docker evaluator. Authors pin greedy/pass@1, NO thinking mode → canonical_condition omitted.

benches.title: CyberGym
benches.description: PoC reproduces the sanitizer crash on a 10-task representative subset (no LLM judge). OpenHands CodeActAgent, max_iter=100 (matches the documented CLI; 10-vs-100 upstream ambiguity). Wall-time 7200s vs authors' 1200s.

benches.title: SEC-bench
benches.description: Differential sanitizer on the patched build, 11-task pre-pulled subset (→50, bd <ISSUE>). Project-canonical = stock smolagents harness at max_turns=30 (the unpatched scaffold an outside observer would reproduce). <ISSUE> measurements are the project's harness-patch uplift, marked as harness-axis deviations from canonical and surfaced in the max/ceiling view. ALL cells are still harness-divergent from upstream authors (who use SWE-agent/OpenHands/Aider), so absolute numbers are not directly cross-comparable to published author baselines.

benches.title: CVE-Bench
benches.description: Live exploit vs containerized stack, 8-outcome /done oracle (Inspect AI + ReAct). Authors pin max_messages=30 and the project matches it — the one Pool A bench with NO budget deviation. Project-canonical is thinking-off (the open-weight harness-bandage default); thinking-on runs show as a marked deviation in canonical view and as the ceiling in the max view. Frontier models (natively thinking-on) will be marked as deviating on the thinking axis — accurate to the condition, not a quality judgement.

benches.title: ExploitBench-14
benches.description: 16-flag capability bitmap, ≥T1 reach (ExploitBench, arXiv:2605.14153). Authors' canonical = 300 turns / ≥128K ctx. The project's 100-turn screening runs are a deviation AND structurally zero (agents never reach grade() under ~300 turns) — publish only 300-turn cells. Deferred (rental wall-time prohibitive); column stays TBD.
